page.title: Footnote: Other legislation affecting buildings
page.p: In addition to any applicable provisions of this Code, there are a number of other legislative technical requirements and policies affecting the design, construction and/or performance of buildings that practitioners may need to be aware of, including, but not necessarily limited to, the following list. Additional legislative instruments such as regulations, codes and standards may exist under the legislation listed.

section.title: Administering Agency
section.p: Department of Health, Disability and Ageing

section.title: Relevant Legislation
section.p: Aged Care Act 2024

section.title: Administering Agency
section.p: Department of Finance, section 27 insofar as it relates to the declaration of land in the Australian Capital Territory to be National Land where the land is required for Commonwealth purposes other than for the special purposes of Canberra as the National Capital.
Department of Infrastructure, Transport, Regional Development, Communications, Sports and the Arts, except to the extent administered by the Minister for Finance

section.title: Relevant Legislation
section.p: National Capital Plan (established under the Australian Capital Territory (Planning and Land Management) Act 1988)

section.title: Administering Agency
section.p: Department of Infrastructure, Transport, Regional Development, Communications, Sports and the Arts

section.title: Relevant Legislation
section.p: Parliament Act 1974
Australian Capital Territory National Land (Leased) Ordinance 2022
Australian Capital Territory National Land (Unleased) Ordinance 2022

section.title: Administering Agency
section.p: Department of Infrastructure, Transport, Regional Development, Communications, Sports and the Arts

section.title: Relevant Legislation
section.p: Christmas Island Act 1958
Cocos (Keeling) Islands Act 1955

section.title: Administering Agency
section.p: Department of Infrastructure, Transport, Regional Development, Communications, Sports and the Arts

section.title: Relevant Legislation
section.p: Australian Postal Corporation Act 1989
National Transmission Network Sale Act 1998
Telecommunications Act 1997
Telstra Corporation Act 1991
Telecommunications (Consumer Protection and Service Standards) Act 1999

section.title: Relevant Policy
section.p: Telecommunications Infrastructure in New Developments (TIND) policy

section.title: Administering Agency
section.p: Department of Defence

section.title: Relevant Legislation
section.p: Defence Act 1903

section.title: Relevant Regulations
section.p: Defence Regulation 2016, Part 11A

section.title: Relevant Codes, Standards and Publications
section.p: Manual of Fire Protection Engineering
Building Works Manual
Manual of Infrastructure Engineering - Electrical
The Defence Estate Quality Management System (DEQMS) https://www.defence.gov.au/EstateManagement/

section.title: Administering Agency
section.p: Attorney-General’s Department

section.title: Relevant Legislation
section.p: Disability (Access to Premises - Buildings) Standards 2010
Disability Discrimination Act 1992
Disability Standards for Accessible Public Transport 2002
Disability Standards for Education 2005, noting this:

ul.li: does not affect the application of premises standards to building work (including construction of a new building) undertaken as an adjustment or part of an adjustment; and
does not require the building work to meet specifications more onerous than those required by the premises standards.

section.title: Administering Agency
section.p: Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water

section.title: Relevant Legislation
section.p: Environmental Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Act 1999
Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Regulations 2025

section.title: Relevant Policy
section.p: Energy Efficiency in Government Operations (2007)
Significant impact guidelines – matters of national environmental significance

section.title: Administering Agency
section.p: Department of Infrastructure, Transport, Regional Development, Communications, Sports and the Arts

section.title: Relevant Legislation
section.p: Airports Act 1996
Airports Regulations 2024
Airports (Building Control) Regulations 1996
Airports (Control of On-Airport Activities) Regulations 1997
Airports (Environment Protection) Regulations 1997
Airports (Protection of Airspace) Regulations 1996

section.title: Administering Agency
section.p: Department of Infrastructure, Transport, Regional Development, Communications, Sports and the Arts

section.title: Relevant Legislation
section.p: Jervis Bay Territory Acceptance Act 1915

section.title: Administering Agency
section.p: Department of Employment and Workplace Relations

section.title: Relevant Legislation
section.p: Work Health and Safety Act 2011
Work Health and Safety Regulations 2011

section.title: Administering Agency
section.p: Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water (Australian Antarctic Division)

section.title: Relevant Legislation
section.p: Antarctic Treaty (Environment Protection) Act 1980
Antarctic Treaty (Environment Protection) (Environmental Impact Assessment) Regulations 1993
Antarctic Treaty (Environment Protection) (Waste Management) Regulations 1994
Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Act 1999
Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Regulations 2025

section.p: Environment Protection and Management Ordinance 1987 (HIMI)
Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Act 1999
Heard Island and McDonald Islands Marine Reserve management plan in operation under the Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Act 1999
Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Regulations 2025

section.title: Administering Agency
section.p: Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water

section.title: Relevant Legislation
section.p: Environment Protection and Management Ordinance 1987 (HIMI)
Antarctic Treaty (Environment Protection) (Environmental Impact Assessment) Regulations 1993
Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Act 1999
Heard Island and McDonald Islands Marine Reserve management plan in operation under the Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Act 1999
Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Regulations 2025

section.title: Administering Agency
section.p: Director of National Parks, Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water Portfolio

section.title: Relevant Legislation
section.p: Commonwealth Reserve management plans in operation under the Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Act 1999
Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Act 1999
Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Regulations 2025

section.title: 15. Commonwealth funding for building work
Administering Agency
section.p: Department of Employment and Workplace Relations
The Federal Safety Commissioner (FSC)
Federal Safety Commissioner Act 2022

ul.li: Federal Safety Commissioner (Accreditation Scheme) Rules 2023 (WHS Accreditation Scheme)
Building and Construction Industry (Improving Productivity) (Federal Safety Officers) General Directions 2017

section.title: Administering Agency
section.p: Department of Employment and Workplace Relations
Office of the Federal Safety Commissioner

section.title: Relevant Legislation
section.p: Federal Safety Commissioner Act 2022

ul.li: Building and Construction Industry (Improving Productivity) (Federal Safety Officers) General Directions 2017

ol.li: The Federal Safety Commissioner Act is concerned with the regulation of Workplace Relations in the building industry, it also establishes the functions of the Federal Safety Commissioner (FSC).

The Federal Safety Commissioner Act legislates additional requirements for those building contractors undertaking Commonwealth funded building work through the Work Health and Safety Accreditation Scheme (WHS Accreditation Scheme), administered by the FSC. Of direct relevance to the NCC: Provisions in Federal Safety Commissioner Act include a requirement that the Federal Safety Commissioner audits building contractors’ compliance with the NCC in relation to building materials, which is a condition of accreditation under the WHS Accreditation Scheme.
